source_paper_title: Upper Bounds for All and Max-Gain Policy Iteration Algorithms on Deterministic MDPs
context:
Page 31, Section 6 (Summary and outlook), first bullet under “Our results give rise to several questions for further investigation.”

It remains unknown if such a Hamiltonian path through the set of policies exists for any MDP with \(k \ge 3\) actions. A concrete first step in pursuit of a “Hamiltonian” lower bound could be to attempt constructing a 3-state, 3-action MDP in which all 27 policies can be visited by PI.
title: Existence of an MDP with \(k\ge 3\) actions whose policy-improvement DAG contains a Hamiltonian path
background_latex:
- An MDP is a tuple \(M=(S,A,T,R,\gamma)\) where \(S\) is a finite set of states with \(|S|=n\), \(A\) is a finite set of actions with \(|A|=k\), \(T:S\times A\times S\to[0,1]\) is a transition kernel, \(R:S\times A\to\mathbb{R}\) is a reward function, and \(\gamma\in[0,1)\) is a discount factor.
- A (deterministic Markov) policy is a mapping \(\pi:S\to A\). The set of all policies is \(\Pi\), with \(|\Pi|=k^n\).
- For a policy \(\pi\), its value function \(V^{\pi}:S\to\mathbb{R}\) is the expected discounted return starting from each state when following \(\pi\).
- For policies \(\pi,\pi'\in\Pi\), write \(\pi\prec\pi'\) if \(V^{\pi}(s)\le V^{\pi'}(s)\) for all \(s\in S\) and \(V^{\pi}(s)<V^{\pi'}(s)\) for at least one \(s\in S\).
- A one-step policy-improvement move from \(\pi\) to \(\pi'\) is any update that changes \(\pi\) at one or more states to actions with strictly positive gain (so that \(\pi\prec\pi'\) holds).
- The policy-improvement directed acyclic graph (PI-DAG) of \(M\) is the digraph \(D=(\Pi,E)\) with an arc \((\pi,\pi')\in E\) whenever \(\pi'\) can be obtained from \(\pi\) by a single policy-improvement step.
- A Hamiltonian path in \(D\) is a directed path that visits every vertex of \(D\) exactly once, i.e., it has length \(|\Pi|-1=k^n-1\).
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether there exists an MDP \(M=(S,A,T,R,\gamma)\) with \(|S|=n\ge 2\) and \(|A|=k\ge 3\) such that the PI-DAG \(D=(\Pi,E)\) of \(M\) contains a directed Hamiltonian path (equivalently, there exists an ordering \(\pi_1,\pi_2,\dots,\pi_{k^n}\) of all policies \(\Pi\) with \((\pi_i,\pi_{i+1})\in E\) for every \(1\le i<k^n\)).
Problem (concrete first step highlighted by the authors): Construct such an example for \(n=3\) and \(k=3\), i.e., an MDP with \(|S|=3\) and \(|A|=3\) whose PI-DAG has a Hamiltonian path that visits all \(3^3=27\) policies.

- A (deterministic Markov) policy is a mapping \(\pi:S\to A\). The set of all policies is \(\Pi\), with \(|\Pi|=k^n\).
- For a policy \(\pi\), its value function \(V^{\pi}:S\to\mathbb{R}\) is the expected discounted return starting from each state when following \(\pi\).
